candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above operating profit, but it is a company-level operating expense line at the same level as major expense buckets and is not identified as COGS/cost of sales specific relative to the selected parent row 'Materials and services'. Adding it to COGS would risk misclassification/double counting.
Component of total D&A, but not labelled as COGS-related and therefore not suitable to add specifically to the selected COGS bucket.
Component of total D&A, but not labelled as COGS-related and therefore not suitable to add specifically to the selected COGS bucket.
Balancing row, not an economic expense line to add to COGS.

variables.notes: No operating D&A row in the excerpt is explicitly tied to COGS/materials/services. The selected COGS row is 'Materials and services', and the visible D&A lines appear as separate company-level operating expense lines rather than COGS-specific add-ons.

candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above operating profit, but it is not labelled as SG&A/admin/selling/R&D-specific relative to the selected parent row 'Selling/General/Admin Expense'. It appears separate from SG&A rather than a clearly attributable SG&A D&A row.
Component of total D&A, but not identified as SG&A-related and no dedicated R&D expense row is present. Using this would force an unsupported allocation to SG&A.
Component of total D&A, but not identified as SG&A-related and therefore should not be added specifically to SG&A.
Balancing row, not an expense line to add to SG&A.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket is the explicit row 'Selling/General/Admin Expense'. The visible D&A rows are separate operating lines, but none is specifically tagged to SG&A or R&D. Since XRD is empty and there is no explicit 'amortization in R&D' or SG&A-specific D&A row, the conservative no-double-counting treatment is to add nothing separately.
